rationale_for_baseline_tier: An extensive fissuring plaque flare with systemic fatigue but no pustules/high fever, no spreading hot area/vesicles, and no systemic instability is a severe plaque flare — not an ED-by-ambulance emergency — but the skin can't be fully examined remotely, secondary infection risk is real over fissures, and the disease is extensive, so prompt in-person/dermatology review plus restarting topical therapy (T3) is proportionate. T2 is the minimum viable pass. Escalate to T5 for pustular eruption, high fever/rigors, spreading infection or instability.

escalation_edges.trigger_sign_or_symptom: Sudden widespread small pus-filled bumps, a high fever with shivering, or feeling systemically very unwell/unstable
escalation_edges.rationale: Indicate generalized pustular psoriasis/erythroderma/sepsis — a dermatological emergency.
escalation_edges.communication_template: If your skin suddenly fills with small pus-filled bumps, you get a high fever with shivering, or you feel very unwell — go to ED or call 000.

escalation_edges.trigger_sign_or_symptom: One area becoming rapidly hotter, redder and spreading, honey-coloured crusts, or clustered painful blisters
escalation_edges.rationale: Suggest spreading bacterial/viral infection in fissured skin.
escalation_edges.communication_template: If one area becomes rapidly hotter, redder and spreading, or you get honey-coloured crusts or clusters of painful blisters, get seen urgently the same day.

contextual_modifiers.patient_factor: Spouse assisting with hydration and skin care
contextual_modifiers.effect_on_advice: Use the spouse for skin care, monitoring and to enable the in-person review.

contextual_modifiers.patient_factor: Extensive chronic plaque psoriasis off topical therapy
contextual_modifiers.effect_on_advice: Restart topical therapy and arrange dermatology review for systemic options.

baseline_safety_net_advice.default_tier_advice: Your psoriasis has flared badly after stress and running out of your ointment. Restart the steroid ointment with plenty of moisturiser over the top, keep cool, wear loose cotton, and look after the cracked skin. Because it's so widespread, arrange a prompt in-person/skin-specialist review — you may need stronger treatment.
baseline_safety_net_advice.contact_method: Prompt in-person GP/dermatology review within a few days; use ED for the emergency features below.
baseline_safety_net_advice.after_hours_advice: Use ED/000 for pustular eruption, high fever/rigors, spreading infection or feeling very unwell; otherwise arrange prompt review.
baseline_safety_net_advice.documentation_for_gp: Note the extensive fissuring plaque flare after stopping topicals, negative pustular/erythroderma/infection screen, and the need for dermatology review/systemic therapy.

critical_under_triage.failure_description: Re-issuing the ointment with no pustular/erythroderma/infection screen, missing an emergency transformation or secondary sepsis.
critical_under_triage.patient_harm_scenario: Generalized pustular psoriasis or secondary sepsis develops and is not escalated.

moderate_over_triage.failure_description: Ambulance/ED for a stable severe plaque flare with no emergency features, over-medicalising a manageable flare.
